title: RED capture + authoritative enumeration of failures by category
head_note: HEAD is 1 commit ahead of dispatch-contract's stated d32cd12. The intervening commit (3306ded) modifies only continuum/autonomous/vuln-source-parity-v1-plan/ docs (sibling-milestone planning); it does not touch any source code or test files. Therefore the M1 RED capture at 3306ded is functionally identical to a capture at d32cd12. Verified via `git diff --stat d32cd12..HEAD` — only 2 files changed, both under vuln-source-parity-v1-plan/.

build_status.interpretation: Re-confirms the planning reframe: build is GREEN at HEAD. No phantom-build-failure scope shift.

implemented: reports/M1-red-capture.txt — full cargo test --workspace --features semantic --release stdout/stderr (15969 lines, 226 total failures including 4 doctests).

reports/M1-doctest-red-capture.txt — separate cargo test --doc capture (informational; 4 doctest failures captured here AND in M1-red-capture.txt; the dispatch-contract requested both for redundancy).

reports/M1-archived-cmd-test-enumeration.json — Cat-A enumeration: 165 entries across 11 test files, classified by 17-element archived CLI token set {cfg, dfg, ssa, gvn, alias, dominators, live-vars, abstract-interp, arch, behavioral, bounds, diff-impact, equivalence, maintainability, mutability, purity, secrets}. `search` EXCLUDED per validator_mandate m1_classification_by_clap_name (active SmartSearch alias).

reports/M1-orthogonal-real-failures.json — Cat-C enumeration: 41 unique entries (42 raw, 1 dup test_search_invalid_regex appears in 2 binaries) with root_cause/disposition/fix_sketch derived from panic excerpts. All 41 disposition=FIX.
reports/M1-out-of-scope-confirmation.json — Cat-B confirmation: exactly 33 entries from crates/tldr-cli/tests/vuln_migration_v1_red.rs, sibling_milestone='vuln-source-parity-v1' for all.

tests.rationale: M1 is investigation-only; no source/test code modifications per dispatch-contract.

reconciliation.method: Sum classification rows from M1-red-capture.txt failures: |Cat-A failing| + |Cat-B| + |Cat-C raw| + |Doctest| should equal total_raw_failures. Note: Cat-A enumeration has 165 total entries but only 147 of them appear in current failure set (18 are #[ignore]'d or otherwise pass). The reconciliation uses the FAILING subset of Cat-A.
reconciliation.cat_a_passing_rows_explanation: These tests use archived CLI tokens but did not appear in the failure set. Most are #[ignore]-attributed (e.g., test_bounds_interval_tracking has #[ignore = "bounds command not yet implemented"]). They are still Cat-A by classification (their test code references archived CLI subcommands) and remain M3 deletion targets per the dispatch-contract's M3 ATOMIC scope.

loc_delta_note: Target was 30 (small JSON artifacts). Actual is 0 LoC of source-code edits — all artifacts are in continuum/autonomous/, no source-code touched per M1 spec.
discoveries: Build is GREEN at HEAD (cargo build --workspace --tests --features semantic exit 0). The investigation/premortem reframe holds.
Total live failures = 226 = 222 unit/integration + 4 doctest. Reconciliation arithmetic is exact (delta=0).

Cat-A enumeration produced 165 entries (vs premortem expectation 218-225). The lower count comes from a stricter inclusion rule: a test fn must (a) call cargo_bin!('tldr')/tldr_cmd()/tldr_assert_cmd() AND (b) reference an archived token as a literal '"<token>"' in its body. The premortem's higher estimate likely double-counted call sites within single test fns (multiple .args calls in one fn collapse to ONE deletion target). Per validator_mandate m1_enumeration_authoritative_over_plan_estimate, 165 is binding.

18 Cat-A entries are #[ignore]'d (they reference archived tokens but don't actually run); they are still M3 deletion targets because their test code is no longer compatible with the active CLI surface.

Cat-C is 41 unique entries (vs dispatch-contract estimate of 16-25). The gap reflects more orthogonal real regressions than expected — most are real bugs (smells default-include-tests behavior changed; SmartSearch JSON schema changed; coverage parsers broken on empty input; cognitive-complexity counts else-branches; tree-sitter-kotlin/swift unsupported-language assertions broken; etc.). All 41 are FIX disposition; no DELETE.

3 unit failure names are duplicated across binaries: test_ssa_memory_flag (2x — Cat-A), test_ssa_help (2x — Cat-A), test_search_invalid_regex (2x — Cat-C, in cli_tests.rs and cli_search_context_tests.rs).

4 doctest failures match the dispatch-contract's M2 spec exactly: callgraph::cross_file_types::FuncIndexProxy (line 1027), kotlin::KotlinHandler::parse_import_node (line 74), luau::LuauHandler::extract_aliased_require (line 217), surface::triggers::extract_name_triggers (line 15). M2 ready to proceed.

tldr_assert_cmd() (in addition to tldr_cmd()) is a CLI-helper used by some tests (contracts_test.rs, daemon_test.rs, patterns_test.rs, remaining_test.rs). Tests using only this helper would have been missed by a naive cargo_bin-only scan.

compliance.no_gaming_or_bypassing: All 41 Cat-C entries have FIX dispositions backed by panic-derived root causes. None are suppressed, weakened, or stubbed.
compliance.category_b_out_of_scope: Verified — exactly 33 Cat-B entries from vuln_migration_v1_red.rs, all assigned sibling_milestone='vuln-source-parity-v1'.
compliance.m1_enumeration_authoritative: M1 enumeration (165 Cat-A) is binding; per validator_mandate the +/- 5 tolerance applies between this enumeration and the eventual M3 actual delete count, not between the dispatch-contract narrative and this enumeration.
compliance.no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: Confirmed — no remote ops, no Cargo.toml version edits, no cargo publish.

next_milestone: M2 — fix the 4 doctest failures (RED→GREEN) per dispatch-contract §M2 green_files specification.
